steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank falls between 'cinema' and 'her film.' The phrase before the blank — 'joined the growing number of Indigenous women adding their voices to North American cinema' — is a complete independent clause. The phrase after — 'her film, a selection of the Native Women in Film Festival, is about the art of painter and musician Rick Bartow' — is also a complete independent clause. Two independent clauses must be separated by a period, semicolon, or a comma with a coordinating conjunction.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'cinema, her': joins two independent clauses with only a comma — a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice B — 'cinema. Her': places a period between the two independent clauses, correctly separating them into two complete sentences. ✓
  • Choice C — 'cinema and her': 'and' is a coordinating conjunction but is not preceded by a comma, and it creates an awkward construction that does not properly join the clauses. ✗
  • Choice D — 'cinema her': no punctuation between two independent clauses — a run-on sentence. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
